title: Vercel AI SDK Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and techniques for using the Vercel AI SDK effectively.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the AI's responses.
commands.scenario: When generating creative content or ideas where variability is desired.

commands.description: Selects the GPT-4o model and enables streaming responses.
commands.scenario: When you need real-time updates for a chatbot application.

commands.command: Think step by step. Then verify your answer.
commands.description: Encourages the AI to reason through problems methodically.
commands.scenario: When building a code review bot that needs deterministic output.

commands.description: Limits the maximum number of tokens in the AI's response.
commands.scenario: When you need concise answers to save on processing costs.

commands.description: Sets the context length for the AI model.
commands.scenario: When handling large documents or conversations that require more context.

commands.description: Instructs the AI to format responses with bullet points for better readability.
commands.scenario: When generating documentation or reports that need clear formatting.

commands.description: Reduces randomness for more deterministic responses.
commands.scenario: When generating legal or technical documents that require precision.

commands.description: Selects the GPT-3.5 model for processing.
commands.scenario: When cost efficiency is more important than the latest model features.

commands.description: Enables verbose logging for debugging purposes.
commands.scenario: When troubleshooting issues with AI response handling.

commands.command: Use examples to clarify your request.
commands.description: Helps the AI understand the task by providing examples.
commands.scenario: When the AI needs to perform a complex task with specific requirements.

commands.description: Sets a timeout for API requests to prevent hanging.
commands.scenario: When integrating the AI into a time-sensitive application.

commands.description: Adjusts the penalty for repeating phrases in the AI's response.
commands.scenario: When you want to encourage diversity in the AI's output.

commands.description: Sets the API key for authentication with Vercel AI services.
commands.scenario: When initializing the SDK to start making requests.

commands.description: Forces the model to call a specific tool immediately, bypassing conversational filler.
Callback function within streamText to intercept and process individual delta chunks for custom UI animations or logging.
Enables strict schema adherence for structured outputs, ensuring the model returns valid JSON matching a Zod schema.

commands.command: system: 'You are a JSON generator. Output ONLY raw JSON without markdown code blocks.'
commands.description: Prompt engineering pattern to prevent the LLM from wrapping structured responses in ```json blocks, reducing parsing errors.
Enables multi-step tool calling, allowing the agent to call a tool, observe the result, and call another tool in a single user turn.
Enables type-safe, streamed structured data generation using Zod schemas for seamless UI integration.
Enables built-in observability and tracing to monitor LLM latency and token usage in production.
Fine-tune agentic behavior: 'auto' lets the model decide, 'required' forces tool usage, and 'none' disables tools.
Callback function to handle the complete response, perfect for logging token usage or triggering post-generation side effects.
Pattern for injecting dynamic application state or retrieved RAG documents directly into the prompt template.
